# Self-Assessment + Consent Portal Review

**Date:** 2026-04-13
**Generated by:** Gemini (auto-generated meeting notes)
**Attendees:** Grisha Szep, Joseph Austerweil, Fiza Razik, Miho Shinada

> The content was auto-generated on April 13, 2026, 09:57 AM JST, and may contain errors.

## Summary

The team refined the consent workflow and technical installation process for upcoming student tool deployment via research.

**Consent workflow and testing:** The team successfully walked through the installation flow and consent procedures, identifying a specific bug where PDF exports fail in Safari. Participants confirmed that research and educational consent toggles operate independently, allowing granular control over data sharing.

**System governance and logistics:** Members finalized the timeline for student account distribution and established the protocol for whitelist management. They decided to delay the official consent rollout to the second week to ensure administrative readiness.

**Technical readiness and support:** Decision made to replace the initial insights panel with a placeholder until sufficient data is collected. The team established support channels to handle authentication troubleshooting and browser inconsistencies during the upcoming class sessions.

## Details

**Upcoming Travel and Moving Plans:** Grisha Szep confirmed that they would be flying out that evening for a business trip to the US. Joseph Austerweil mentioned that they are slowly moving, with Alyssa starting a new company, and they are excited to be in Tokyo over the next month. Joseph Austerweil also shared that they are moving near Disneyland in Miami, which Miho Shinada noted is a fun place to go.

**Upcoming Lectures and Staff Roles:** Joseph Austerweil noted that the following day's lectures would be the first time that Grisha Szep and Fiza Razik would be giving the consent procedures. Grisha Szep is traveling across "the bigger pond" in about six hours. Joseph Austerweil thanked Grisha Szep for getting everything ready right before intense travel.

**System Vigilance and Admin Access:** Grisha Szep requested that everyone maintain vigilance to stress test the system and report any bugs. To ensure issues can be solved self-sufficiently, Grisha Szep planned to give full admin access to Joseph Austerweil and Ira that day. They also stated that they would review the flow together that day, which would be recorded and turned into markdown for follow-up.

**Consent Procedure Practice:** Joseph Austerweil suggested that Fiza Razik ask questions but noted that they would not be the one explaining things instead of Grisha Szep. Grisha Szep offered to provide a practice version of what they would say. Joseph Austerweil noted that this practice would result in a script created by Gemini.

**Review of Public Web Page and Localization:** Grisha Szep began reviewing the publicly accessible web page, which is available in both English and Japanese. Joseph Austerweil asked if a native speaker had reviewed the Japanese content for accuracy. Miho Shinada volunteered to review the Japanese translation.

**Initial Installation Steps:** Grisha Szep described the initial steps, which include installing the agent log CLI to start sharing cloud code sessions, noting that cloud code must be installed and the cloud account will be detected automatically. The first requirement is to install the agent log CLI, not the cloud code CLI.

**Tool Restrictions and Tracking Scope:** Grisha Szep clarified that the agent log tool is restricted to tracking data specifically for the cloud code CLI. It does not track anything outside of that, including cloud co-work or the web chat, as it is designed for the bare-bones agentic coding tool.

**Metadata and Privacy Concerns:** Grisha Szep mentioned that if users are issued accounts through their organization, they have access to co-work telemetry and can additionally enable tracking of all chat history via the compliance API. Joseph Austerweil expressed discomfort with web chat capturing because it is not part of the ethics board approval, and they decided not to mention having access to it. The team agreed that they should encourage students to use cloud code over cloud co-work because co-work is opaque to them.

**Data Access and Research Restrictions:** Joseph Austerweil suggested that accessing raw, broad statistics on how much other tools are being used might be helpful without being intrusive. Grisha Szep clarified that cloud co-work is the most opaque, offering only telemetry access, while they have access to everything in the chat if enabled, though Joseph Austerweil noted this is not legally permissible for research purposes. The conversation pivoted back to the installation flow.

**CLA Installation and Verification:** The installation flow involves copying and pasting the CLA install command, followed by running two completion commands, and then typing claude in the terminal. Fiza Razik successfully went through the steps, which included receiving a verification code via email to finalize the login.

**Initial Sharing Consent at the Folder Level:** Following authentication, the system asks, "Would you like to share this session?" and clarifies that logs in that specific folder will be shared. Grisha Szep demonstrated saying no to sharing their home folder, resulting in the message "agent logs not shared for this project". Joseph Austerweil suggested Fiza Razik try the next steps as a participant, which they did.

**Required Informed Consent Process:** After Fiza Razik typed claude, the system displayed a "consent required" message, directing them to a portal to read and sign the informed consent form. Fiza Razik accessed the portal, which required another one-time verification code.

**Educational vs. Research Consent Toggles:** Grisha Szep clarified that educational use is a one-time consent and mandatory, but research use can be changed anytime. Fiza Razik tested consenting only to the educational use portion, which resulted in a PDF hitting the servers for compliance.

**Safari Export Bug Identified:** When Fiza Razik attempted to export the PDF in Safari, a bug was encountered, yielding the error "null is not an object evaluating when.cument". Fiza Razik then tested the flow in Chrome, confirming the export worked correctly and that the bug was Safari-specific. Joseph Austerweil noted that the issue might be present in Ira's IDP export for Safari as well.

**Changing Research Use Consent:** Fiza Razik confirmed that they could toggle the research use setting on and off anytime in the portal. Grisha Szep clarified that the system immediately updates the consent status without needing to re-export documents. Joseph Austerweil clarified that the decision to share the folder itself is local and folder-specific, but the educational/research classification is a global setting.

**Data Backfilling and Deletion Policy:** Fiza Razik asked if turning research use back on would result in previous unshared data being shared. Grisha Szep clarified that data shared for educational use gets copied over to the anonymized research database upon consent. If consent is revoked, the data is flagged for non-use and deletion, which is permanently executed one month after the course finishes, aligning with the consent change cutoff point.

**Folder-Specific Sharing Consent:** The group moved to a new folder named 'assignment,' and Fiza Razik typed claude again. This triggered a project-specific sharing question, and they consented to share the logs for the assignment folder. The footer of the terminal confirmed, "agent log sharing now for educational use and research use".

**Addressing Technical Login Issues:** When Fiza Razik tried to change the consent settings mid-session, they encountered an issue, which Grisha Szep identified as a potential lack of cloud code login, despite being logged into cloud co-work. Grisha Szep instructed them to use claude auth login to resolve this, noting that many people might experience this issue. Miho Shinada and Joseph Austerweil also had their emails added to test the cloud code authentication.

**Browser Inconsistencies and Workarounds:** The PDF export was confirmed to work on Firefox, confirming Safari as the outlier. The team also discussed the need to transition users out of the default "ask for permissions constantly" mode in cloud code, possibly by allowing all tool calls in the global settings.

**Mid-Flow Consent Revocation and Plain Language Commands:** Fiza Razik was able to revoke settings mid-flow using plain English commands. Grisha Szep noted that users can describe their intent in plain English or Japanese to change their consent settings.

**Granular Control and Withdrawal Bug:** Joseph Austerweil advised that confused participants should check the logs tab to see what is included and specifically withdraw consent for a folder there. Upon testing, clicking "withdraw" in the session logs resulted in a "failed to update revocation status" error in both Safari and Chrome, which Grisha Szep noted as a real bug that requires a fix.

**Pre-Course Survey and Research Purpose:** The team reviewed Joseph Austerweil's pre-course survey, which is available in English and Japanese and is part of the research component. Joseph Austerweil explained the research goal is to understand the best way to teach with AI in higher education, as the field struggles to make and evaluate students in the age of AI.

**Data Sharing and Access Limitations:** Grisha Szep clarified that the data is shared only with Chibetch and not with Anthropic. Joseph Austerweil added that the data is published and stored in a de-identified way, so participant identities will never be linked to the data. Access is limited exclusively to the researchers listed on the ethics board, who will not know who has opted in or out of the research.

**Shared Data Details for Claude Code:** Fiza Razik asked about the level of detail shared for cloud code compared to the metadata shared for cloud co-work/chat. Grisha Szep confirmed that the text the user writes and the text received back is shared, but sensitive details, such as file access or private keys, are stripped to protect the machine. This stripping of sensitive metadata applies to both educational and research use.

**Organization Name Protocol:** Fiza Razik inquired about changing the organization name from CIT to Chibetch in the consent form. Joseph Austerweil explained that they cannot change it if CIT was in the ethics protocol unless the Japanese version is the legally binding one, which Fiza Razik confirmed. The team agreed they could use the translation error as protection for the change.

**Timeline and Logistical Adjustments:** The estimated time for the consent process, originally 15 minutes, was noted to be closer to 20-25 minutes. Miho Shinada raised a concern that cloud accounts would not be given out until the 20th or 21st, meaning the consent procedure should be delayed until the second week. Joseph Austerweil agreed, relieving Fiza Razik of the responsibility for the first week.

**Proposed Consent Explanation Flow:** Fiza Razik outlined their planned flow for presenting the consent, starting with the purpose of the study and the mandatory educational use. They planned to then introduce the optional research part, walk through the getting started process, and answer questions about data sharing. Grisha Szep advised against mentioning the funding source but suggested emphasizing that data sharing helps educational players like Chibetch remain competitive.

**Support for Technical Issues:** Fiza Razik requested that Grisha Szep be available to resolve terminal-based issues that might arise during the session. Grisha Szep agreed to provide support for any technical issues. The official contact for technical issues is claude@shebatech.dev.

**Stress Testing and System Reliability Concerns:** Grisha Szep expressed that they are worried about the current system and requested that the team stress test it as much as possible through various means to find all the ways that it can break. Joseph Austerweil offered reassurance, stating that things go wrong in research all the time and that the system being developed should be considered the first version, which means a lot of things will go wrong, but they must also minimize damage related to individuals' personal data.

**Collaborative System Building and Support for Individuals:** The team acknowledged they are building the system together, and Grisha Szep stressed that people should feel free to reach out with any concerns or questions. Joseph Austerweil expressed gratitude for the team's hard work, and Miho Shinada complimented Grisha Szep for building a great system.

**Scheduling for Web 3 Class Preparation:** Fiza Razik proposed that the team meet after the APS meeting tomorrow to assess the outcomes and questions received, which will help tailor the system for the Web 3 class. Joseph Austerweil confirmed the schedule change and supported the idea of meeting after the APS session.

**Student Invitation and Account Upgrade Status:** Miho Shinada inquired about the process for inviting students to the platform, and Grisha Szep clarified that the account upgrade is expected to happen sometime this week when Joanna returns from holiday. The upgrade will enable the team to issue the standard seats, and Grisha Szep confirmed that adding students is easy if they send a list of email addresses.

**Miho Shinada's Admin Account Access:** Grisha Szep advised Miho Shinada to attempt logging into the claude.dev interface using the Google option and the email claude@chibotech.dev to test access to the admin account. Miho Shinada successfully logged in to the admin account named "Bunny" after some troubleshooting regarding the web interface and the incorrect forwarding mechanism.

**Managing User Access and Account Seating:** Grisha Szep explained that the notifications section shows all the pending requests for access, and they are not approving them until they know who is supposed to get access. Miho Shinada can use the provided list or the CSV option for bulk adding email addresses, and an invite link is also available under "ID to access".

**Clarification on Agent Log Authentication:** Joseph Austerweil inquired about the process for the agent logs for the Web 3 students, and Grisha Szep confirmed that the same account used for the Claude account must be used for the agent log side. Grisha Szep also noted that if a user's email is not allow-listed, they receive a message instructing them to contact the provided admin email.

**External User Whitelisting and Email Collection:** Joseph Austerweil suggested that it would be helpful to whitelist external students ahead of time, which Grisha Szep agreed to, provided that they receive specific instructions for students to share the same email they use for their Claude account. It was noted that only external people need to be added, as users with an @chibotech.ac.jp address are automatically included.

**Pre-Class Preparation and Claude Installation:** Fiza Razik asked Joseph Austerweil to ensure that students have already downloaded Claude before the walkthrough, as Kinley was requesting help with the download process. Grisha Szep confirmed that all students' accounts are activated, and the team only needs to walk them through a two-step process to use Claude Code, which is different from Claude Co-work.

**Support for Code Setup and Potential Risks:** Grisha Szep shared the quick start link with Joseph Austerweil for the Claude Code setup, explaining that the simple code copy-paste process should eliminate most problems, and it works on Mac, Linux, and WSL. Grisha Szep warned that Mac users might need to install Xcode, which is a potential risk that might require an additional oneliner.

**Translation and Error Correction for Instructions:** Miho Shinada offered to review the instructions and questionnaires for errors or mistranslations, and Grisha Szep requested that they inform them of any issues as soon as possible, even providing corrections in Japanese via Slack. Grisha Szep acknowledged that they did not focus as much on translations due to time constraints.

**Management of the Insights Panel:** Grisha Szep indicated that they plan to replace the "Insights" panel with a "Coming Soon" message to manage user expectations. The rationale is that the system needs to collect at least two weeks' worth of information to generate interesting insights, and the first set of insights will be available after Golden Week.

**Post-Meeting Logistics and Travel:** Joseph Austerweil closed the meeting by wishing Grisha Szep safe travels and thanking them for their availability during a stressful travel time. Grisha Szep confirmed they would take nice pictures and that the Gemini notes will be sent automatically.
